{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011Q1pkWArit9c7vCV5x1r91","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"13f27bc6-2a95-499b-a60b-1170129cc890","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:00:10.161Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HsJBE6Sugn2G89sy6kLo2Z","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Testing\n    32→- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n    33→- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n    34→- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n    35→- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n    36→- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n    37→- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n    38→- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n    39→- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n    40→- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n    41→\n    42→## Browser Testing\n    43→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    44→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    45→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    46→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    47→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    48→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    49→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    50→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    51→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    52→\n    53→## Deployment\n    54→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    55→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    56→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    57→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    58→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    59→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    60→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    61→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    62→\n    63→## React + SQLite\n    64→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    65→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    66→\n    67→## Security\n    68→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    69→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    70→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    71→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    72→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    73→\n    74→## Working Directory\n    75→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    76→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    77→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    78→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"c3a42df7-b15c-4e27-82f1-5c01e5331ea1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:00:10.576Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Testing\n- Vitest is the test runner: `cd app && npm test`\n- Tests live in `app/__tests__/` with helpers in `app/__tests__/helpers/`\n- Use `vi.hoisted()` with inline `require(\"better-sqlite3\")` to create in-memory DBs for vi.mock factories\n- vi.mock is hoisted above imports — can't reference variables declared after it; use `vi.hoisted()` for shared state\n- Mock bcryptjs to avoid slow hashing (cost factor 12) in unit tests\n- Mock `@/lib/session` to avoid `next/headers` dependency outside request context\n- Route uses `q` param for search (not `search`) and `meeting_format` for format filter (not `format`)\n- ESLint allows `require()` in test files (needed for vi.hoisted pattern)\n- `RequestInit` type needs cast to `NextRequest` for NextRequest constructor compatibility\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n","numLines":78,"startLine":1,"totalLines":78}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01PacqQfryEGFf4YFTUVDR3k","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Registered Platforms\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub\n     4→- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n     5→- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n     6→- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n     7→\n     8→## Production Deployment\n     9→- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n    10→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    11→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n    12→- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n    13→- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n    14→- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n    15→- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n    16→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"9d12051d-40fb-495b-9c63-19e93fe71017","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:00:11.075Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/platforms.md","content":"# Registered Platforms\n\n## GitHub\n- Repo: TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- Project: Bookmate (#1)\n- Auth: GH_TOKEN in .claude-beat/.env\n\n## Production Deployment\n- **Host**: VPS at 89.167.127.85 (IPv6: 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1)\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx, ecosystem.config.js)\n- **Port**: 3000 (direct, no reverse proxy)\n- **Deploy**: `./scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **No HTTPS**: No domain configured, no certbot/nginx (no root access)\n- **DB**: SQLite at app/data/ (persistent on disk)\n","numLines":16,"startLine":1,"totalLines":16}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BxhytB2HHzAJGCFzEc3g2r","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 23:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 6: found and fixed 5 bugs including a critical logout failure. Created issue #86, implemented fixes in PR #87, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (112min uptime), 197 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Emails**: Issue #74 notifications (already closed), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    12→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #86: Code review round 6 (PR #87 — merged + deployed)\n    17→\n    18→5 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (CRITICAL) — In `logout/route.ts` and `me/route.ts`, `session.destroy()` is async but was called without `await`. The cookie was never cleared, so users remained logged in until the 7-day session expiry. Fixed by adding `await`.\n    21→\n    22→2. **`is_full` not reset on group resize** (CRITICAL) — When an author increased `maxGroupSize` on a previously-full listing, `is_full` stayed `1` and the listing remained permanently invisible in browse. Fixed by checking member count vs new size in the PATCH handler and resetting `is_full = 0` when appropriate.\n    23→\n    24→3. **Missing email subject cases** (IMPORTANT) — `telegram_ready` and `discord_ready` notification types fell through to generic \"Bookmate: Notification\" subject. Added proper subject lines.\n    25→\n    26→4. **Discord webhook `listingId` validation** (IMPORTANT) — `handleLink` accepted non-integer/negative `listingId` values. Added integer validation consistent with other routes.\n    27→\n    28→5. **BookSearch fetch race condition** (IMPORTANT) — Added `AbortController` to cancel in-flight Open Library API requests on component unmount or rapid query changes, preventing stale responses from overwriting newer results.\n    29→\n    30→All existing 197 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    31→\n    32→## Issues Monitored\n    33→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n    34→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    35→\n    36→## Next Session Priority\n    37→1. Check production health\n    38→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    39→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"ab9345a6-6d96-4b39-8133-04526178ed68","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:00:17.161Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-23-2300.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-23 23:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 6: found and fixed 5 bugs including a critical logout failure. Created issue #86, implemented fixes in PR #87, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (112min uptime), 197 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: Issue #74 notifications (already closed), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #86: Code review round 6 (PR #87 — merged + deployed)\n\n5 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (CRITICAL) — In `logout/route.ts` and `me/route.ts`, `session.destroy()` is async but was called without `await`. The cookie was never cleared, so users remained logged in until the 7-day session expiry. Fixed by adding `await`.\n\n2. **`is_full` not reset on group resize** (CRITICAL) — When an author increased `maxGroupSize` on a previously-full listing, `is_full` stayed `1` and the listing remained permanently invisible in browse. Fixed by checking member count vs new size in the PATCH handler and resetting `is_full = 0` when appropriate.\n\n3. **Missing email subject cases** (IMPORTANT) — `telegram_ready` and `discord_ready` notification types fell through to generic \"Bookmate: Notification\" subject. Added proper subject lines.\n\n4. **Discord webhook `listingId` validation** (IMPORTANT) — `handleLink` accepted non-integer/negative `listingId` values. Added integer validation consistent with other routes.\n\n5. **BookSearch fetch race condition** (IMPORTANT) — Added `AbortController` to cancel in-flight Open Library API requests on component unmount or rapid query changes, preventing stale responses from overwriting newer results.\n\nAll existing 197 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NkiW4L2gfJqdYdjeJgoZah","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 22:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Added comprehensive test coverage for all 8 Telegram and Discord API routes — the last untested route files in the codebase. Created issue #84, implemented tests in PR #85, CI passed, merged to main.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (52min uptime), 128 tests pass\n     8→- **PM2 logs**: \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors from external probing (known, not a bug)\n     9→- **CI**: Green on main\n    10→- **Open PRs**: None\n    11→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~4 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    12→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    13→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    14→\n    15→## Work Completed\n    16→\n    17→### Issue #84: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord routes (PR #85 — merged)\n    18→\n    19→69 new tests across 8 test files:\n    20→\n    21→1. **listing-telegram.test.ts** (7 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format, success\n    22→2. **listing-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format (discord.gg + discord.com/invite), success\n    23→3. **listing-auto-telegram.test.ts** (9 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, deep link success, generation failure\n    24→4. **listing-auto-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, invite URL success\n    25→5. **telegram-setup.test.ts** (8 tests) — secret validation, bot config, webhook URL, getBotInfo failure, setWebhook failure, success, GET status\n    26→6. **telegram-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, empty update, /start with payload, pending_telegram_groups, /link (linked/unlinked user), /link_ID (owner/non-owner), non-group ignore, invite fallback\n    27→7. **discord-setup.test.ts** (5 tests) — secret validation, bot config, getBotInfo failure, success\n    28→8. **discord-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, unknown type, PING/PONG, missing params, listing not found, not full, already set, channel creation + linking, provided channelId, creation/invite failure\n    29→\n    30→**Total test Created issue #84, implemented tests in PR #85, CI passed, merged to main.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (52min uptime), 128 tests pass\n- **PM2 logs**: \"Failed to find Server Action\" errors from external probing (known, not a bug)\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~4 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #84: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord routes (PR #85 — merged)\n\n69 new tests across 8 test files:\n\n1. **listing-telegram.test.ts** (7 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format, success\n2. **listing-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, validation, author-only, full-group guard, link format (discord.gg + discord.com/invite), success\n3. **listing-auto-telegram.test.ts** (9 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, deep link success, generation failure\n4. **listing-auto-discord.test.ts** (8 tests) — auth, bot config, validation, author-only, full-group, already-set, invite URL success\n5. **telegram-setup.test.ts** (8 tests) — secret validation, bot config, webhook URL, getBotInfo failure, setWebhook failure, success, GET status\n6. **telegram-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, empty update, /start with payload, pending_telegram_groups, /link (linked/unlinked user), /link_ID (owner/non-owner), non-group ignore, invite fallback\n7. **discord-setup.test.ts** (5 tests) — secret validation, bot config, getBotInfo failure, success\n8. **discord-webhook.test.ts** (11 tests) — secret validation, unknown type, PING/PONG, missing params, listing not found, not full, already set, channel creation + linking, provided channelId, creation/invite failure\n\n**Total test count: 128 → 197 (54% increase)**\n\nAll tests pass, ESLint clean, build passes, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~4 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01DkqpZpz3hKxxpbrS55drYF","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","name":"Agent","input":{"subagent_type":"feature-dev:code-reviewer","description":"Deep code review of app","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app. Focus on:\n\n1. **Bugs and logic errors** - anything that could cause incorrect behavior\n2. **Security vulnerabilities** - SQL injection, XSS, auth bypasses, etc.\n3. **Performance issues** - N+1 queries, missing indexes, expensive operations\n4. **Error handling gaps** - unhandled promises, missing error boundaries\n5. **UX issues in code** - broken flows, missing validations\n\nThis is a Next.js 15 App Router project with SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session auth, and Tailwind CSS.\n\nKey directories:\n- app/app/ - pages and API routes\n- app/components/ - React components\n- app/lib/ - core libraries (db, session, notifications)\n\nThere have already been 6 rounds of code review that fixed many issues. Focus on finding NEW issues that haven't been caught yet. Report only HIGH-CONFIDENCE bugs or issues. Don't report style preferences or minor suggestions.\n\nDO NOT write any code. Just research and report findings."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":967,"cache_read_input_tokens":30518,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":967},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"b9b4f555-05e1-4eb9-8d33-9eca65483dcd"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HDMw9ySWqHA5F1D6TQB5yL","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import Database from \"better-sqlite3\";\n     2→import path from \"path\";\n     3→import fs from \"fs\";\n     4→\n     5→const DB_DIR = process.env.DB_DIR || path.join(process.cwd(), \"data\");\n     6→const DB_PATH = path.join(DB_DIR, \"bookmate.db\");\n     7→\n     8→// Ensure data directory exists\n     9→if (!fs.existsSync(DB_DIR)) {\n    10→  fs.mkdirSync(DB_DIR, { recursive: true });\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→const db = new Database(DB_PATH);\n    14→\n    15→// Enable WAL mode for better concurrent performance\n    16→db.pragma(\"journal_mode = WAL\");\n    17→db.pragma(\"foreign_keys = ON\");\n    18→\n    19→// Initialize schema\n    20→db.exec(`\n    21→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n    22→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    23→    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,\n    24→    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,\n    25→    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n    26→    bio TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    27→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n    28→  );\n    29→\n    30→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listings (\n    31→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    32→    author_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    33→    book_title TEXT NOT NULL,\n    34→    book_author TEXT NOT NULL,\n    35→    book_cover_url TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    36→    book_olid TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    37→    language TEXT DEFAULT 'English',\n    38→    reading_pace TEXT NOT NULL,\n    39→    start_date TEXT NOT NULL,\n    40→    meeting_format TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(meeting_format IN ('voice', 'text', 'mixed')),\n    41→    max_group_size INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(max_group_size >= 2 AND max_group_size <= 20),\n    42→    telegram_link TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    43→    is_full INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    44→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    45→    FOREIGN KEY (author_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    46→  );\n    47→\n    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_members (\n    49→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    50→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    51→    joined_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    52→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id, user_id),\n    53→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    54→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    55→  );\n    56→\n    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_genres (\n    58→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    59→    genre TEXT NOT NULL,\n    60→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id, genre),\n    61→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notifications (\n    65→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    66→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    67→    listing_id INTEGER,\n    68→    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    69→    message TEXT NOT NULL,\n    70→    is_read INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n    71→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    72→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n    73→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n    74→  );\n    75→\n    76→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS listing_applications (\n    77→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n    78→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    79→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    80→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'\n    81→      CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected')),\n    82→    message TEXT DEFAULT '',\n    83→    applied_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n    84→    decided_at TEXT,\n    85→    UNIQUE(listing_id, user_id),\n    86→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n    87→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n    88→  );\n    89→`);\n    90→\n    91→// Idempotent migration: add requires_approval column\n    92→try {\n    93→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN requires_approval INTEGER DEFAULT 0\");\n    94→} catch {\n    95→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n    96→}\n    97→\n    98→// Idempotent migration: add platform_preference column\n    99→try {\n   100→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN platform_preference TEXT DEFAULT 'telegram'\");\n   101→} catch {\n   102→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→// Idempotent migration: add discord_link column\n   106→try {\n   107→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_link TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   108→} catch {\n   109→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   110→}\n   111→\n   112→// Idempotent migration: add discord_channel_id column\n   113→try {\n   114→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN discord_channel_id TEXT DEFAULT ''\");\n   115→} catch {\n   116→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   117→}\n   118→\n   119→// Idempotent migration: add telegram_chat_id column for auto-telegram feature\n   120→try {\n   121→  db.exec(\"ALTER TABLE listings ADD COLUMN telegram_chat_id INTEGER\");\n   122→} catch {\n   123→  // Column already exists — safe to ignore\n   124→}\n   125→\n   126→// Tables for Telegram bot integration\n   127→db.exec(`\n   128→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_telegram_groups (\n   129→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   130→    telegram_chat_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   131→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   132→    PRIMARY KEY (listing_id),\n   133→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id)\n   134→  );\n   135→\n   136→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_chats (\n   137→    chat_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n   138→    chat_title TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   139→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS telegram_user_links (\n   143→    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   144→    telegram_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   145→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   146→    PRIMARY KEY (user_id),\n   147→    UNIQUE (telegram_user_id),\n   148→    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   149→  );\n   150→`);\n   151→\n   152→// Rating and Reputation system\n   153→db.exec(`\n   154→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ratings (\n   155→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n   156→    listing_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   157→    rater_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   158→    rated_user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,\n   159→    score INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(score >= 1 AND score <= 5),\n   160→    comment TEXT DEFAULT '',\n   161→    created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),\n   162→    UNIQUE(listing_id, rater_id, rated_user_id),\n   163→    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id),\n   164→    FOREIGN KEY (rater_id) REFERENCES users(id),\n   165→    FOREIGN KEY (rated_user_id) REFERENCES users(id)\n   166→  );\n   167→`);\n   168→\n   169→// Performance indexes on high-frequency foreign key columns\n   170→db.exec(`\n   171→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_members_user_id ON listing_members(user_id);\n   172→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_id ON notifications(user_id);\n   173→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ratings_rated_user_id ON ratings(rated_user_id);\n   174→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listings_author_id ON listings(author_id);\n   175→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_listing_applications_listing_status ON listing_applications(listing_id, status);\n   176→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notifications_user_unread ON notifications(user_id, is_read);\n   177→`);\n   178→\n   179→export default db;\n   180→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"b51c05ee-0968-45bb-8036-e045c772dc81","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:31.601Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Svj7uDwMjJRNWFwNFme9fj","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"bc81da1d-871e-4359-bce2-7fa43e3b3331","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:32.003Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013waJwh1k2MBMNEEkT8vEsA","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,\n    81→      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n    82→      creates_join_request: false,\n    83→    }\n    84→  );\n    85→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    86→    return res.result.invite_link;\n    87→  }\n    88→  return null;\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n    93→ */\n    94→export async function exportChatInviteLink(\n    95→  chatId: number | string\n    96→): Promise<string | null> {\n    97→  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    98→    chat_id: chatId,\n    99→  });\n   100→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n   101→    return res.result;\n   102→  }\n   103→  return null;\n   104→}\n   105→\n   106→/**\n   107→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n   108→ */\n   109→export async function sendMessage(\n   110→  chatId: number | string,\n   111→  text: string\n   112→): Promise<boolean> {\n   113→  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n   114→    chat_id: chatId,\n   115→    text,\n   116→    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n   117→  });\n   118→  return res.ok;\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→/**\n   122→ * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n   123→ */\n   124→export async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n   125→  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n   126→    url,\n   127→    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n   128→  });\n   129→  return res.ok;\n   130→}\n   131→\n   132→/**\n   133→ * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n   134→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   135→ */\n   136→export function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n   137→  payload: string\n   138→): number | null {\n   139→  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   140→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   141→}\n   142→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"f4f9b265-d4d5-4afc-befd-04482dbb4f06","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:32.113Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_015KdZnPsTLwEgyHjkmyhgTE","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015PDtAehN12QZTPmiDtTUjo","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5111,"cache_read_input_tokens":11537,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5111,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":36,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"ee78055a-d147-4416-8fb8-54e1e401f0d6"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015PDtAehN12QZTPmiDtTUjo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    18→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    19→\n    20→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    21→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→/**\n    25→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    26→ */\n    27→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    28→  endpoint: string,\n    29→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    30→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    31→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    32→  try {\n    33→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    34→      method,\n    35→      headers: {\n    36→        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n    37→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    38→      },\n    39→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    40→    });\n    41→\n    42→    if (!res.ok) {\n    43→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    44→      return {\n    45→        ok: false,\n    46→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    47→      };\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    51→    return { ok: true, data };\n    52→  } catch (err) {\n    53→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    54→  }\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→/**\n    58→ * Get bot user info.\n    59→ */\n    60→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    61→  id: string;\n    62→  username: string;\n    63→} | null> {\n    64→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    65→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→/**\n    69→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    70→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    71→ *\n    72→ * Permission bits:\n    73→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    74→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    75→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    76→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    77→ * Total: 3089\n    78→ */\n    79→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    80→  const permissions = 3089;\n    81→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n    82→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n    83→}\n    84→\n    85→/**\n    86→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n    87→ */\n    88→export async function createTextChannel(\n    89→  guildId: string,\n    90→  name: string,\n    91→  topic?: string\n    92→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n    93→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    94→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n    95→    \"POST\",\n    96→    {\n    97→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n    98→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n    99→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   100→    }\n   101→  );\n   102→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→/**\n   106→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   107→ */\n   108→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   109→  channelId: string,\n   110→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   111→): Promise<string | null> {\n   112→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   113→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   114→    \"POST\",\n   115→    {\n   116→      max_age: maxAge,\n   117→      max_uses: 0,\n   118→      unique: true,\n   119→    }\n   120→  );\n   121→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   122→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   123→  }\n   124→  return null;\n   125→}\n   126→\n   127→/**\n   128→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   129→ */\n   130→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   131→  channelId: string,\n   132→  content: string\n   133→): Promise<boolean> {\n   134→  const res = await callApi(\n   135→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   136→    \"POST\",\n   137→    { content }\n   138→  );\n   139→  return res.ok;\n   140→}\n   141→\n   142→/**\n   143→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   144→ */\n   145→export async function getGuild(\n   146→  guildId: string\n   147→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   148→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   149→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   150→  );\n   151→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   152→}\n   153→\n   154→/**\n   155→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   156→ */\n   157→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   158→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   159→> {\n   160→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   161→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   162→  );\n   163→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   164→}\n   165→\n   166→/**\n   167→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   168→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   169→ */\n   170→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   171→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   172→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   173→}\n   174→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"9884c86f-cf24-4a32-a7d0-015f10fd4966","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:34.424Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MeG2UHeWSxSSxZsr8rBdDE","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import bcrypt from \"bcryptjs\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     5→import { checkRateLimit } from \"@/lib/rate-limit\";\n     6→\n     7→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n     8→  try {\n     9→    const { email, password } = await req.json();\n    10→\n    11→    if (!email || !password) {\n    12→      return NextResponse.json(\n    13→        { error: \"Email and password are required\" },\n    14→        { status: 400 }\n    15→      );\n    16→    }\n    17→\n    18→    // Rate limit: 5 login attempts per email+IP per 15 minutes\n    19→    const ip = req.headers.get(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0]?.trim() || \"unknown\";\n    20→    const rateLimitKey = `login:${ip}:${email.toLowerCase().trim()}`;\n    21→    const { allowed, retryAfter } = checkRateLimit(rateLimitKey, 5);\n    22→    if (!allowed) {\n    23→      return NextResponse.json(\n    24→        { error: \"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\" },\n    25→        { status: 429, headers: { \"Retry-After\": String(retryAfter) } }\n    26→      );\n    27→    }\n    28→\n    29→    const user = db\n    30→      .prepare(\"SELECT id, email, password_hash, display_name FROM users WHERE email = ?\")\n    31→      .get(email.toLowerCase().trim()) as\n    32→      | { id: number; email: string; password_hash: string; display_name: string }\n    33→      | undefined;\n    34→\n    35→    if (!user) {\n    36→      return NextResponse.json(\n    37→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    38→        { status: 401 }\n    39→      );\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    const valid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password_hash);\n    43→    if (!valid) {\n    44→      return NextResponse.json(\n    45→        { error: \"Invalid email or password\" },\n    46→        { status: 401 }\n    47→      );\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    const session = await getSession();\n    51→    session.userId = user.id;\n    52→    session.email = user.email;\n    53→    session.displayName = user.display_name;\n    54→    await session.save();\n    55→\n    56→    return NextResponse.json({\n    57→      user: {\n    58→        id: user.id,\n    59→        email: user.email,\n    60→        displayName: user.display_name,\n    61→      },\n    62→    });\n    63→  } catch (error) {\n    64→    console.error(\"Login error:\", error);\n    65→    return NextResponse.json(\n    66→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    67→      { status: 500 }\n    68→    );\n    69→  }\n    70→}\n    71→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"87ab652b-18f3-4a6f-8dd0-d020d697151b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:35.302Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EkGJBAo9j42CPRaKJFgdUw","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyApplicationReceived } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json(\n    31→      { error: \"You cannot apply to your own listing\" },\n    32→      { status: 400 }\n    33→    );\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (!listing.requires_approval) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json(\n    38→      { error: \"This group does not require approval. Join directly.\" },\n    39→      { status: 400 }\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    45→  }\n    46→\n    47→  // Wrap membership check + application in a transaction to prevent TOCTOU races\n    48→  const applyTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    49→    // Check if already a member\n    50→    const isMember = db\n    51→      .prepare(\"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\")\n    52→      .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    53→\n    54→    if (isMember) {\n    55→      return { error: \"You are already a member\", status: 400 };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    // Re-check is_full inside transaction\n    59→    const currentListing = db\n    60→      .prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    61→      .get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n    62→\n    63→    if (currentListing?.is_full) {\n    64→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", status: 400 };\n    65→    }\n    66→\n    67→    // Check if already applied (pending)\n    68→    const existingApp = db\n    69→      .prepare(\n    70→        \"SELECT id, status FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    71→      )\n    72→      .get(listingId, session.userId) as { id: number; status: string } | undefined;\n    73→\n    74→    if (existingApp) {\n    75→      if (existingApp.status === \"pending\") {\n    76→        return { error: \"You have already applied\", status: 400 };\n    77→      }\n    78→      if (existingApp.status === \"approved\") {\n    79→        return { error: \"You are already approved\", status: 400 };\n    80→      }\n    81→      // If rejected, allow re-application by updating status back to pending\n    82→      db.prepare(\n    83→        \"UPDATE listing_applications SET status = 'pending', decided_at = NULL, applied_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?\"\n    84→      ).run(existingApp.id);\n    85→\n    86→      return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    // Insert new application\n    90→    db.prepare(\n    91→      \"INSERT INTO listing_applications (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    92→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    93→\n    94→    return { error: null, status: 200 };\n    95→  });\n    96→\n    97→  const result = applyTransaction();\n    98→\n    99→  if (result.error) {\n   100→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: result.status });\n   101→  }\n   102→\n   103→  // Notify outside transaction (fire-and-forget)\n   104→  notifyApplicationReceived(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   105→\n   106→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n   107→}\n   108→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"02da13e7-99d4-4f31-8807-fd7590ef4014","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:35.601Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NPYxuTPgYazUD9Tfz4UpQ7","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→import { notifyListingAuthor, notifyGroupFull } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     5→\n     6→export async function POST(\n     7→  _req: NextRequest,\n     8→  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }\n     9→) {\n    10→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    11→  if (!session) {\n    12→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    13→  }\n    14→\n    15→  const { id } = await params;\n    16→  const listingId = parseInt(id, 10);\n    17→  if (isNaN(listingId)) {\n    18→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid listing ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    19→  }\n    20→\n    21→  const listing = db\n    22→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    23→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    24→\n    25→  if (!listing) {\n    26→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    27→  }\n    28→\n    29→  // Author is already a member — don't allow re-joining\n    30→  if (listing.author_id === session.userId) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are the organizer of this group\" }, { status: 400 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  if (listing.requires_approval) {\n    35→    return NextResponse.json(\n    36→      { error: \"This group requires approval. Please apply instead.\" },\n    37→      { status: 400 }\n    38→    );\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (listing.is_full) {\n    42→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"This group is already full\" }, { status: 400 });\n    43→  }\n    44→\n    45→  // Check if already a member\n    46→  const existing = db\n    47→    .prepare(\n    48→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    )\n    50→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    51→\n    52→  if (existing) {\n    53→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"You are already a member\" }, { status: 400 });\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Check if user has a pending application (e.g., requires_approval was toggled off)\n    57→  const pendingApp = db\n    58→    .prepare(\n    59→      \"SELECT 1 FROM listing_applications WHERE listing_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND status = 'pending'\"\n    60→    )\n    61→    .get(listingId, session.userId);\n    62→\n    63→  if (pendingApp) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"You already have a pending application for this group\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  // Wrap count check + insert in a transaction to prevent race conditions\n    71→  const joinTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n    72→    const { count } = db\n    73→      .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    74→      .get(listingId) as { count: number };\n    75→\n    76→    if (count >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    77→      return { error: \"This group is already full\", count };\n    78→    }\n    79→\n    80→    db.prepare(\n    81→      \"INSERT INTO listing_members (listing_id, user_id) VALUES (?, ?)\"\n    82→    ).run(listingId, session.userId);\n    83→\n    84→    const newCount = count + 1;\n    85→    if (newCount >= (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n    86→      db.prepare(\"UPDATE listings SET is_full = 1 WHERE id = ?\").run(listingId);\n    87→    }\n    88→\n    89→    return { error: null, count: newCount };\n    90→  });\n    91→\n    92→  const result = joinTransaction();\n    93→\n    94→  if (result.error) {\n    95→    return NextResponse.json({ error: result.error }, { status: 400 });\n    96→  }\n    97→\n    98→  // Notify outside the transaction (fire-and-forget)\n    99→  notifyListingAuthor(listingId, session.displayName || \"Someone\");\n   100→\n   101→  if (result.count === (listing.max_group_size as number)) {\n   102→    notifyGroupFull(listingId);\n   103→  }\n   104→\n   105→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, memberCount: result.count });\n   106→}\n   107→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"9a4f8a5b-fb61-4d40-a818-290a96d22979","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:01:42.102Z"}
