83→    };\n    84→  }, [query]);\n    85→\n    86→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    87→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    88→    onSelect({\n    89→      title: book.title,\n    90→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    91→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    92→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    93→        : \"\",\n    94→      olid,\n    95→    });\n    96→    setQuery(book.title);\n    97→    setOpen(false);\n    98→    setActiveIndex(-1);\n    99→  };\n   100→\n   101→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n   102→    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n   103→\n   104→    switch (e.key) {\n   105→      case \"ArrowDown\":\n   106→        e.preventDefault();\n   107→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n   108→        break;\n   109→      case \"ArrowUp\":\n   110→        e.preventDefault();\n   111→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n   112→        break;\n   113→      case \"Enter\":\n   114→        e.preventDefault();\n   115→        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n   116→          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n   117→        }\n   118→        break;\n   119→      case \"Escape\":\n   120→        setOpen(false);\n   121→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n   122→        break;\n   123→    }\n   124→  };\n   125→\n   126→  // Scroll active option into view\n   127→  useEffect(() => {\n   128→    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n   129→      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n   130→      if (activeEl) {\n   131→        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n   132→      }\n   133→    }\n   134→  }, [activeIndex]);\n   135→\n   136→  const activeDescendant =\n   137→    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n   138→      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n   139→      : undefined;\n   140→\n   141→  return (\n   142→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n   143→      <input\n   144→        type=\"text\"\n   145→        className=\"input-field\"\n   146→        value={query}\n   147→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n   148→        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n   149→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n   150→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n   151→        role=\"combobox\"\n   152→        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n   153→        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n   154→        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   155→        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n aria-live=\"polite\"\n   165→        >\n   166→          Searching...\n   167→        </div>\n   168→      )}\n   169→\n   170→      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n   171→        <div\n   172→          ref={listboxRef}\n   173→          id=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   174→          role=\"listbox\"\n   175→          aria-label=\"Book search results\"\n   176→          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n   177→          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n   178→        >\n   179→          {results.map((book, index) => (\n   180→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Wu82MmzJ4DM3bexKw88Tj3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { getSession } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     3→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await getSession();\n     7→\n     8→  if (!session.userId) {\n     9→    return NextResponse.json({ user: null });\n    10→  }\n    11→\n    12→  const user = db\n    13→    .prepare(\"SELECT id, email, display_name, bio FROM users WHERE id = ?\")\n    14→    .get(session.userId) as\n    15→    | { id: number; email: string; display_name: string; bio: string }\n    16→    | undefined;\n    17→\n    18→  if (!user) {\n    19→    await session.destroy();\n    20→    return NextResponse.json({ user: null });\n    21→  }\n    22→\n    23→  // Include unread notification count to avoid a second round-trip from the Navbar\n    24→  const unread = db\n    25→    .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\")\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    user: {\n    30→      id: user.id,\n    31→      email: user.email,\n    32→      displayName: user.display_name,\n    33→      bio: user.bio,\n    34→      unreadNotificationCount: unread.count,\n    35→    },\n    36→  });\n    37→}\n    38→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"36ca15f6-981e-4940-9a8b-914464e2f312","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:02:58.224Z"}

OFFSET ?`\n    77→    )\n    78→    .all(...params, PAGE_SIZE, offset);\n    79→\n    80→  return NextResponse.json({"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"505bd596-a934-4329-82fd-018e1e70fce7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:04:34.373Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01V3tmF7JCFkCGzmChEFNPnc","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→\n     4→const PAGE_SIZE = 20;\n     5→\n     6→export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {\n     7→  const { searchParams } = request.nextUrl;\n     8→\n     9→  const q = searchParams.get(\"q\")?.trim() || \"\";\n    10→  const meetingFormat = searchParams.get(\"meeting_format\") || \"\";\n    11→  const readingPace = searchParams.get(\"reading_pace\") || \"\";\n    12→  const startDateFrom = searchParams.get(\"start_date_from\") || \"\";\n    13→  const sort = searchParams.get(\"sort\") || \"newest\";\n    14→  const includePast = searchParams.get(\"include_past\") === \"true\";\n    15→  const pageParam = parseInt(searchParams.get(\"page\") || \"1\", 10);\n    16→  const page = isNaN(pageParam) || pageParam < 1 ? 1 : Math.min(pageParam, 1000);\n    17→\n    18→  const conditions: string[] = [\"l.is_full = 0\"];\n    19→  const params: (string | number)[] = [];\n    20→\n    21→  // Apply date floor: use the more restrictive of today or startDateFrom\n    22→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    23→  const hasStartDateFilter = startDateFrom && /^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2}$/.test(startDateFrom);\n    24→\n    25→  if (!includePast) {\n    26→    // Use whichever is later: today or the explicit start_date_from filter\n    27→    const effectiveDate = hasStartDateFilter && startDateFrom > today ? startDateFrom : today;\n    28→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    29→    params.push(effectiveDate);\n    30→  } else if (hasStartDateFilter) {\n    31→    // includePast is true, but user wants a specific start date floor\n    32→    conditions.push(\"l.start_date >= ?\");\n    33→    params.push(startDateFrom);\n    34→  }\n    35→\n    36→  if (q && q.length <= 300) {\n    37→    conditions.push(\"(l.book_title LIKE ? OR l.book_author LIKE ?)\");\n    38→    params.push(`%${q}%`, `%${q}%`);\n    39→  }\n    40→\n    41→  if (meetingFormat && [\"voice\", \"text\", \"mixed\"].includes(meetingFormat)) {\n    42→    conditions.push(\"l.meeting_format = ?\");\n    43→    params.push(meetingFormat);\n    44→  }\n    45→\n    46→  if (readingPace && readingPace.length <= 200) {\n    47→    conditions.push(\"l.reading_pace LIKE ?\");\n    48→    params.push(`%${readingPace}%`);\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  const whereClause = conditions.join(\" AND \");\n    52→\n    53→  let orderClause = \"l.created_at DESC\";\n    54→  if (sort === \"oldest\") orderClause = \"l.created_at ASC\";\n    55→  else if (sort === \"start_date\") orderClause = \"l.start_date ASC\";\n    56→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"b91c6fc7-4a56-413a-86cc-66685efaeb6b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:04:45.115Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ANSvWUDcuiwWHqNLkQ3GPC","type":"tool_result","content":"    56→async function handleLink(body: {\n    57→  guildId: string;\n    58→  listingId: number;\n    59→  channelId?: string;\n    60→}) {\n    61→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    62→\n    63→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    71→    return NextResponse.json(\n    72→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n    73→      { status: 400 }\n    74→    );\n    75→  }\n    76→\n    77→  const listing = db\n    78→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    79→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    80→\n    81→  if (!listing) {\n    82→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    83→  }\n    84→\n    85→  if (!listing.is_full) {"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"5f4e54db-e68e-496f-aa2d-361ca37e9984","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:04:58.181Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Y5rP4Mk1d9p8wjjy5RPqKm","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|----------|-------|------|------------|\n| 1 | Critical | HTML injection via book title into Telegram HTML-mode messages | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:339`, `lib/telegram.ts:116` | 95 |\n| 2 | Critical | Discord `guildId`/`channelId` not validated before URL path construction | `app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts:61`, `lib/discord.ts:89-135` | 90 |\n| 3 | Important | Telegram `/start listing_ID` handler has no author ownership check | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:164-169` | 88 |\n| 4 | Important | `is_full` flag logic race / uses two separate DB reads in `maxGroupSize` update | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:231-238` | 85 |\n| 5 | Important | `discord_link` missing from `/api/profile/reading` response | `app/api/profile/reading/route.ts:28-48` | 82 |\n| 6 | Important | No rate limiting on join/apply endpoints allows notification flooding and DB abuse | `app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, `apply/route.ts` | 80 |\n\nThe most urgent items are #1 (HTML injection into Telegram) and #3 (unauthenticated listing hijacking via the Telegram `/start` command), as both can be exploited by parties outside the application with no authentication required."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a4c778e7f55542c26 (use SendMessage with to: 'a4c778e7f55542c26' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 112277\ntool_uses: 72\nduration_ms: 300041</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"1d208531-1c8f-4b91-83ad-5849e6823437","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:06:21.594Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Do a thorough code review of the Bookmate Next.js application in /root/Projects/bookmate/app. Focus on:\n\n1. **Bugs and logic errors** - anything that could cause incorrect behavior\n2. **Security vulnerabilities** - SQL injection, XSS, auth bypasses, etc.\n3. **Performance issues** - N+1 queries, missing indexes, expensive operations\n4. **Error handling gaps** - unhandled promises, missing error boundaries\n5. **UX issues in code** - broken flows, missing validations\n\nThis is a Next.js 15 App Router project with SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session auth, and Tailwind CSS.\n\nKey directories:\n- app/app/ - pages and API routes\n- app/components/ - React components\n- app/lib/ - core libraries (db, session, notifications)\n\nThere have already been 6 rounds of code review that fixed many issues. Focus on finding NEW issues that haven't been caught yet. Report only HIGH-CONFIDENCE bugs or issues. Don't report style preferences or minor suggestions.\n\nDO NOT write any code. Just research and report findings.","agentId":"a4c778e7f55542c26","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|----------|-------|------|------------|\n| 1 | Critical | HTML injection via book title into Telegram HTML-mode messages | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:339`, `lib/telegram.ts:116` | 95 |\n| 2 | Critical | Discord `guildId`/`channelId` not validated before URL path construction | `app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts:61`, `lib/discord.ts:89-135` | 90 |\n| 3 | Important | Telegram `/start listing_ID` handler has no author ownership check | `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:164-169` | 88 |\n| 4 | Important | `is_full` flag logic race / uses two separate DB reads in `maxGroupSize` update | `app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts:231-238` | 85 |\n| 5 | Important | `discord_link` missing from `/api/profile/reading` response | `app/api/profile/reading/route.ts:28-48` | 82 |\n| 6 | Important | No rate limiting on join/apply endpoints allows notification flooding and DB abuse | `app/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts`, `apply/route.ts` | 80 |\n\nThe most urgent items are #1 (HTML injection into Telegram) and #3 (unauthenticated listing hijacking via the Telegram `/start` command), as both can be exploited by parties outside the application with no authentication required."}],"totalDurationMs":300041,"totalTokens":112277,"totalToolUseCount":72,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2173,"cache_read_input_tokens":109716,"output_tokens":387,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2173},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BohYLTbL6wxeWtx8CGdDrb","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n     6→ * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n     7→ * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n    11→ * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n    12→ * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n    13→ * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n    18→\n    19→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    20→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n    21→}\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n    25→ */\n    26→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    27→  method: string,\n    28→  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n    29→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n    30→  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    31→    method: \"POST\",\n    32→    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    33→    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n    34→  });\n    35→  return res.json();\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Get bot info (username, id).\n    40→ */\n    41→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    42→  id: number;\n    43→  username: string;\n    44→  first_name: string;\n    45→} | null> {\n    46→  const res = await callApi<{\n    47→    id: number;\n    48→    username: string;\n    49→    first_name: string;\n    50→  }>(\"getMe\");\n    51→  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→/**\n    55→ * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n    56→ * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n    57→ *\n    58→ * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n    59→ *\n    60→ * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n    61→ * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n    62→ */\n    63→export async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n    64→  listingId: number\n    65→): Promise<string | null> {\n    66→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    67→  if (!bot) return null;\n    68→  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n    69→}\n    70→\n    71→/**\n    72→ * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n    73→ */\n    74→export async function createChatInviteLink(\n    75→  chatId: number | string\n    76→): Promise<string | null> {\n    77→  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    78→    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    79→    {\n    80→      chat_id: chatId,\n    81→      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n    82→      creates_join_request: false,\n    83→    }\n    84→  );\n    85→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    86→    return res.result.invite_link;\n    87→  }\n    88→  return null;\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n    93→ */\n    94→export async function exportChatInviteLink(\n    95→  chatId: number | string\n    96→): Promise<string | null> {\n    97→  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    98→    chat_id: chatId,\n    99→  });\n   100→  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n   101→    return res.result;\n   102→  }\n   103→  return null;\n   104→}\n   105→\n   106→/**\n   107→ * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n   108→ */\n   109→export async function sendMessage(\n   110→  chatId: number | string,\n   111→  text: string\n   112→): Promise<boolean> {\n   113→  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n   114→    chat_id: chatId,\n   115→    text,\n   116→    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n   117→  });\n   118→  return res.ok;\n   119→}\n   120→\n   121→/**\n   122→ * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n   123→ */\n   124→export async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n   125→  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n   126→    url,\n   127→    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n   128→  });\n   129→  return res.ok;\n   130→}\n   131→\n   132→/**\n   133→ * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n   134→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   135→ */\n   136→export function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n   137→  payload: string\n   138→): number | null {\n   139→  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   140→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   141→}\n   142→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"a10e14f4-e46c-4c7b-b93a-32f4f31fab5c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:06:41.307Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/telegram.ts","content":"/**\n * Telegram Bot API integration for automatic group creation.\n *\n * When TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set, the system can:\n * 1. Generate deep links for one-click group creation\n * 2. Detect when the bot is added to a group (via webhook)\n * 3. Auto-generate and save invite links\n *\n * Flow:\n * - Author clicks \"Create Telegram Group\" → opens deep link in Telegram\n * - Telegram prompts author to create a group with the bot\n * - Bot receives `my_chat_member` update via webhook\n * - Bot creates an invite link and saves it to the listing\n */\n\nconst BOT_TOKEN = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\nconst TELEGRAM_API = `https://api.telegram.org/bot${BOT_TOKEN}`;\n\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Call the Telegram Bot API.\n */\nasync function callApi<T = unknown>(\n  method: string,\n  params?: Record<string, unknown>\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; result?: T; description?: string }> {\n  const res = await fetch(`${TELEGRAM_API}/${method}`, {\n    method: \"POST\",\n    headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    body: JSON.stringify(params || {}),\n  });\n  return res.json();\n}\n\n/**\n * Get bot info (username, id).\n */\nexport async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n  id: number;\n  username: string;\n  first_name: string;\n} | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{\n    id: number;\n    username: string;\n    first_name: string;\n  }>(\"getMe\");\n  return res.ok ? res.result! : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a deep link that opens Telegram and prompts the user\n * to create a new group with the bot already added.\n *\n * Format: https://t.me/BOT_USERNAME?startgroup=listing_LISTING_ID\n *\n * When the user creates the group, Telegram adds the bot and\n * sends a `my_chat_member` update to our webhook.\n */\nexport async function generateGroupDeepLink(\n  listingId: number\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n  if (!bot) return null;\n  return `https://t.me/${bot.username}?startgroup=listing_${listingId}`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an invite link for a chat where the bot is an admin.\n */\nexport async function createChatInviteLink(\n  chatId: number | string\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ invite_link: string }>(\n    \"createChatInviteLink\",\n    {\n      chat_id: chatId,\n      name: \"Bookmate Reading Group\",\n      creates_join_request: false,\n    }\n  );\n  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    return res.result.invite_link;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the existing invite link for a chat (fallback).\n */\nexport async function exportChatInviteLink(\n  chatId: number | string\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<string>(\"exportChatInviteLink\", {\n    chat_id: chatId,\n  });\n  if (res.ok && res.result) {\n    return res.result;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Send a message to a Telegram chat.\n */\nexport async function sendMessage(\n  chatId: number | string,\n  text: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\"sendMessage\", {\n    chat_id: chatId,\n    text,\n    parse_mode: \"HTML\",\n  });\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Set up webhook URL for receiving Telegram updates.\n */\nexport async function setWebhook(url: string): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\"setWebhook\", {\n    url,\n    allowed_updates: [\"my_chat_member\", \"message\"],\n  });\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse the listing ID from a /start command payload.\n * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n */\nexport function parseListingIdFromPayload(\n  payload: string\n): number | null {\n  const match = payload.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n}\n","numLines":142,"startLine":1,"totalLines":142}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013ehWxSr1bEJPTaCzoZKETD","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     8→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n     9→\n    10→/**\n    11→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    12→ *\n    13→ * This endpoint handles:\n    14→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    15→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    16→ *\n    17→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    18→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    19→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    20→ */\n    21→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    22→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    23→  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    24→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    26→  }\n    27→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    28→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    29→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    30→  }\n    31→\n    32→  try {\n    33→    const body = await req.json();\n    34→    const { type } = body;\n    35→\n    36→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    37→      return handleLink(body);\n    38→    }\n    39→\n    40→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    41→    if (type === 1) {\n    42→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    43→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    44→    }\n    45→\n    46→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    47→  } catch (err) {\n    48→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    49→    return NextResponse.json(\n    50→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    51→      { status: 500 }\n    52→    );\n    53→  }\n    54→}\n    55→\n    56→async function handleLink(body: {\n    57→  guildId: string;\n    58→  listingId: number;\n    59→  channelId?: string;\n    60→}) {\n    61→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    62→\n    63→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    64→    return NextResponse.json(\n    65→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    66→      { status: 400 }\n    67→    );\n    68→  }\n    69→\n    70→  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    71→    return NextResponse.json(\n    72→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n    73→      { status: 400 }\n    74→    );\n    75→  }\n    76→\n    77→  const listing = db\n    78→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    79→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    80→\n    81→  if (!listing) {\n    82→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    83→  }\n    84→\n    85→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    86→    return NextResponse.json(\n    87→      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n    88→      { status: 400 }\n    89→    );\n    90→  }\n    91→\n    92→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    93→    return NextResponse.json(\n    94→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n    95→      { status: 400 }\n    96→    );\n    97→  }\n    98→\n    99→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n   100→\n   101→  // If no channel specified, create one\n   102→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n   103→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n   104→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n   105→      .toLowerCase()\n   106→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n   107→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n   108→\n   109→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n   110→      guildId,\n   111→      channelName,\n   112→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n   113→    );\n   114→\n   115→    if (!channel) {\n   116→      return NextResponse.json(\n   117→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n   118→        { status: 500 }\n   119→      );\n   120→    }\n   121→\n   122→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n   123→  }\n   124→\n   125→  // Create invite link\n   126→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   127→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   128→    return NextResponse.json(\n   129→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   130→      { status: 500 }\n   131→    );\n   132→  }\n   133→\n   134→  // Save to DB\n   135→  db.prepare(\n   136→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   137→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   138→\n   139→  // Notify all members\n   140→  const members = db\n   141→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   142→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   143→\n   144→  for (const member of members) {\n   145→    createNotification(\n   146→      member.user_id,\n   147→      listingId,\n   148→      \"discord_ready\",\n   149→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   150→    );\n   151→  }\n   152→\n   153→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   154→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   155→    targetChannelId,\n   156→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   157→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   158→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   159→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   160→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   161→  );\n   162→\n   163→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   164→}\n   165→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"3b63cd47-c0b3-4137-a5a1-9f167e4452f6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:06:44.056Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createTextChannel,\n  createChannelInvite,\n  sendChannelMessage,\n} from \"@/lib/discord\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n *\n * This endpoint handles:\n * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n *\n * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const body = await req.json();\n    const { type } = body;\n\n    if (type === \"link\") {\n      return handleLink(body);\n    }\n\n    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    if (type === 1) {\n      // PING - respond with PONG\n      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    }\n\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n  } catch (err) {\n    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n}\n\nasync function handleLink(body: {\n  guildId: string;\n  listingId: number;\n  channelId?: string;\n}) {\n  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n\n  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n  }\n\n  if (!listing.is_full) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (listing.discord_link) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n      { status: 400 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n\n  // If no channel specified, create one\n  if (!targetChannelId) {\n    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n      .toLowerCase()\n      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n\n    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n      guildId,\n      channelName,\n      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n    );\n\n    if (!channel) {\n      return NextResponse.json(\n        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n        { status: 500 }\n      );\n    }\n\n    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n  }\n\n  // Create invite link\n  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n      { status: 500 }\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Save to DB\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"discord_ready\",\n      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send welcome message to the channel\n  await sendChannelMessage(\n    targetChannelId,\n    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n}\n","numLines":165,"startLine":1,"totalLines":165}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01AL2q3U6YR2xkcwU536dKn5","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    18→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    19→\n    20→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    21→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→/**\n    25→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    26→ */\n    27→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    28→  endpoint: string,\n    29→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    30→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    31→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    32→  try {\n    33→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    34→      method,\n    35→      headers: {\n    36→        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n    37→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    38→      },\n    39→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    40→    });\n    41→\n    42→    if (!res.ok) {\n    43→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    44→      return {\n    45→        ok: false,\n    46→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    47→      };\n    48→    }\n    49→\n    50→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    51→    return { ok: true, data };\n    52→  } catch (err) {\n    53→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    54→  }\n    55→}\n    56→\n    57→/**\n    58→ * Get bot user info.\n    59→ */\n    60→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    61→  id: string;\n    62→  username: string;\n    63→} | null> {\n    64→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    65→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    66→}\n    67→\n    68→/**\n    69→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    70→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    71→ *\n    72→ * Permission bits:\n    73→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    74→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    75→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    76→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    77→ * Total: 3089\n    78→ */\n    79→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    80→  const permissions = 3089;\n    81→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n    82→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n    83→}\n    84→\n    85→/**\n    86→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n    87→ */\n    88→export async function createTextChannel(\n    89→  guildId: string,\n    90→  name: string,\n    91→  topic?: string\n    92→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n    93→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    94→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n    95→    \"POST\",\n    96→    {\n    97→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n    98→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n    99→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   100→    }\n   101→  );\n   102→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   103→}\n   104→\n   105→/**\n   106→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   107→ */\n   108→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   109→  channelId: string,\n   110→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   111→): Promise<string | null> {\n   112→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   113→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   114→    \"POST\",\n   115→    {\n   116→      max_age: maxAge,\n   117→      max_uses: 0,\n   118→      unique: true,\n   119→    }\n   120→  );\n   121→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   122→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   123→  }\n   124→  return null;\n   125→}\n   126→\n   127→/**\n   128→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   129→ */\n   130→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   131→  channelId: string,\n   132→  content: string\n   133→): Promise<boolean> {\n   134→  const res = await callApi(\n   135→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   136→    \"POST\",\n   137→    { content }\n   138→  );\n   139→  return res.ok;\n   140→}\n   141→\n   142→/**\n   143→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   144→ */\n   145→export async function getGuild(\n   146→  guildId: string\n   147→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   148→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   149→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   150→  );\n   151→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   152→}\n   153→\n   154→/**\n   155→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   156→ */\n   157→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   158→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   159→> {\n   160→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   161→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   162→  );\n   163→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   164→}\n   165→\n   166→/**\n   167→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   168→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   169→ */\n   170→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   171→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   172→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   173→}\n   174→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"f32b2a57-b25f-40c6-aa94-4fe230070f8b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:06:44.724Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts","content":"/**\n * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n *\n * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n *\n * Flow:\n * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n * - Author adds bot to their server\n * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n */\n\nconst BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\nconst CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\nconst DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n\nexport function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n}\n\n/**\n * Call the Discord REST API.\n */\nasync function callApi<T = unknown>(\n  endpoint: string,\n  method: string = \"GET\",\n  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n  try {\n    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n      method,\n      headers: {\n        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n      },\n      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    });\n\n    if (!res.ok) {\n      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n      return {\n        ok: false,\n        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    return { ok: true, data };\n  } catch (err) {\n    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get bot user info.\n */\nexport async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n  id: string;\n  username: string;\n} | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n *\n * Permission bits:\n * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n * Total: 3089\n */\nexport function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n  const permissions = 3089;\n  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create a text channel in a guild.\n */\nexport async function createTextChannel(\n  guildId: string,\n  name: string,\n  topic?: string\n): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n    \"POST\",\n    {\n      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n    }\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Create an invite for a channel.\n */\nexport async function createChannelInvite(\n  channelId: string,\n  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n): Promise<string | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n    \"POST\",\n    {\n      max_age: maxAge,\n      max_uses: 0,\n      unique: true,\n    }\n  );\n  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n  }\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n */\nexport async function sendChannelMessage(\n  channelId: string,\n  content: string\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const res = await callApi(\n    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n    \"POST\",\n    { content }\n  );\n  return res.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get guild (server) info.\n */\nexport async function getGuild(\n  guildId: string\n): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n */\nexport async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n  { id: string; name: string }[]\n> {\n  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n  );\n  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n */\nexport function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n}\n","numLines":174,"startLine":1,"totalLines":174}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01ESKJFvYYX5peX4w17TTt8W","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→\n    11→/**\n    12→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    13→ *\n    14→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    15→ *\n    16→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    17→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    18→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    19→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    20→ *\n    21→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    22→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    25→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    26→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    27→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    29→  }\n    30→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    31→  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    32→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    33→  }\n    34→\n    35→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    36→  try {\n    37→    update = await req.json();\n    38→  } catch {\n    39→    return NextResponse.json({ error: handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    52→  }\n    53→\n    54→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    55→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    56→}\n    first_name: string;\n    67→  };\n    68→  new_chat_member: {\n    69→    user: {\n    70→      id: number;\n    71→      is_bot: boolean;\n    72→    };\n    73→    status: string;\n    74→  };\n    75→}\n    76→\n    77→interface TelegramMessage {\n    78→  chat: {\n    79→    id: number;\n    80→    type: string;\n    81→    title?: string;\n    82→  };\n    83→  text?: string;\n    84→  from?: {\n    85→    id: number;\n    86→  };\n    87→}\n    88→\n    89→/**\n    90→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    91→ *\n    92→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    93→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    94→ * 1. Creates the group\n    95→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    96→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    97→ *\n    98→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n    99→ */\n   100→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   101→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   102→\n   103→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   104→  if (\n   105→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   106→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   107→  ) {\n   108→    return;\n   109→  }\n   110→\n   111→  // Only process group chats\n   112→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   113→    return;\n   114→  }\n   115→\n   116→  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n   117→  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n   118→  const pending = db\n   119→    .prepare(\n   120→      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n   121→    )\n   122→    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n   123→\n   124→  if (pending) {\n   125→    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n   126→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n   127→      chat.id\n   128→    );\n   129→    return;\n   130→  }\n   131→\n   132→  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n   133→  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n   134→  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n   135→  await sendMessage(\n   136→    chat.id,\n   137→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   138→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   139→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   140→  );\n   141→\n   142→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   143→  try {\n   144→    db.prepare(\n   145→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   146→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   147→  } catch {\n   148→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   149→  }\n   150→}\n   151→\n   152→/**\n   153→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   154→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   155→ */\n   156→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   157→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   158→    return;\n   159→  }\n   160→\n   161→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   162→\n   163→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   164→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   165→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   166→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   167→    if (listingId) {\n   168→      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   169→      return;\n   170→    }\n   171→  }\n   172→\n   173→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   174→  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   175→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   176→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   177→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   178→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   179→      return;\n   180→    }\n   181→\n   182→    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   183→    const linkedUser = db\n   184→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   185→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   186→\n   187→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   188→      await sendMessage(\n   189→        message.chat.id,\n   190→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n   191→          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n   192→      );\n   193→      return;\n   194→    }\n   195→\n   196→    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n   197→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   198→      .prepare(\n   199→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   200→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   201→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   202→         AND author_id = ?\n   203→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   204→      )\n   205→      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   206→\n   207→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   208→      await sendMessage(\n   209→        message.chat.id,\n   210→        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n   211→      );\n   212→      return;\n   213→    }\n   214→\n   215→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   216→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   217→      return;\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   221→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n   222→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   223→    await sendMessage(\n   224→      message.chat.id,\n   225→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   226→    );\n   227→    return;\n   228→  }\n   229→\n   230→  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n   231→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   232→  if (linkMatch) {\n   233→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   234→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   235→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   236→      return;\n   237→    }\n   238→\n   239→    const linkedUser = db\n   240→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   241→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   242→\n   243→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   244→      await sendMessage(\n   245→        message.chat.id,\n   246→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n   247→      );\n   248→      return;\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   252→\n   253→    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n   254→    const listing = db\n   255→      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   256→      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n   257→\n   258→    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n   259→      await sendMessage(\n   260→        message.chat.id,\n   261→        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n   262→      );\n   263→      return;\n   264→    }\n   265→\n   266→    if (!listing.is_full) {\n   267→      await sendMessage(\n   268→        message.chat.id,\n   269→        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n   270→      );\n   271→      return;\n   272→    }\n   273→\n   274→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   275→  }\n   276→}\n   277→\n   278→/**\n   279→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   280→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   281→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   282→ * 3. Notify all members\n   283→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   284→ */\n   285→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   286→  const listing = db\n   287→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   288→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   289→\n   290→  if (!listing) {\n   291→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   292→    return;\n   293→  }\n   294→\n   295→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   296→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   297→    return;\n   298→  }\n   299→\n   300→  // Try to create an invite link\n   301→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   302→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   303→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   304→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   305→  }\n   306→\n   307→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   308→    await sendMessage(\n   309→      chatId,\n   310→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   311→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   312→    );\n   313→    return;\n   314→  }\n   315→\n   316→  // Save to database\n   317→  db.prepare(\n   318→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   319→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   320→\n   321→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   322→  const members = db\n   323→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   324→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   325→\n   326→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   327→  for (const member of members) {\n   328→    createNotification(\n   329→      member.user_id,\n   330→      listingId,\n   331→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   332→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   333→    );\n   334→  }\n   335→\n   336→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   337→  await sendMessage(\n   338→    chatId,\n   339→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   340→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   341→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   342→  );\n   343→}\n   344→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"04d42d46-c6c7-40e3-a9e8-628b27c514ee","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:06:45.340Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts","content":"import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport {\n  createChatInviteLink,\n  exportChatInviteLink,\n  sendMessage,\n  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\nimport { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n\n/**\n * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n *\n * Handles two types of updates:\n *\n * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n *    and save it to the listing.\n *\n * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n */\nexport async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n  }\n  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n  if (headerSecret !== webhookSecret) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n  }\n\n  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n  try {\n    update = await req.json();\n  } catch {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n  }\n\n  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n  if (update.message) {\n    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n  }\n\n  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n}\n\ninterface ChatMemberUpdate {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    title?: string;\n    type: string;\n  };\n  from: {\n    id: number;\n    first_name: string;\n  };\n  new_chat_member: {\n    user: {\n      id: number;\n      is_bot: boolean;\n    };\n    status: string;\n  };\n}\n\ninterface TelegramMessage {\n  chat: {\n    id: number;\n    type: string;\n    title?: string;\n  };\n  text?: string;\n  from?: {\n    id: number;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n *\n * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n * 1. Creates the group\n * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n *\n * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n */\nasync function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n\n  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n  if (\n    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n  ) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Only process group chats\n  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n  const pending = db\n    .prepare(\n      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n    )\n    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n\n  if (pending) {\n    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n      chat.id\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n  await sendMessage(\n    chat.id,\n    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n  );\n\n  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n  try {\n    db.prepare(\n      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n  } catch {\n    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Handle messages in group chats.\n * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n */\nasync function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const text = message.text.trim();\n\n  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n    if (listingId) {\n      await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n  if (text === \"/link\") {\n    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n    if (!telegramUserId) {\n      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n    const linkedUser = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!linkedUser) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n    const unlinkedListings = db\n      .prepare(\n        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n         AND author_id = ?\n         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n      )\n      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const listText = unlinkedListings\n      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${l.book_title}\"`)\n      .join(\"\\n\");\n    await sendMessage(\n      message.chat.id,\n      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n  if (linkMatch) {\n    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n    if (!telegramUserId) {\n      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const linkedUser = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!linkedUser) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n\n    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n    const listing = db\n      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n\n    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!listing.is_full) {\n      await sendMessage(\n        message.chat.id,\n        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n      );\n      return;\n    }\n\n    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n * 1. Generate an invite link\n * 2. Save it to the listing\n * 3. Notify all members\n * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n */\nasync function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n  const listing = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n\n  if (!listing) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Try to create an invite link\n  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n  }\n\n  if (!inviteLink) {\n    await sendMessage(\n      chatId,\n      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n        \"then try again with /link\"\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Save to database\n  db.prepare(\n    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n\n  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n  const members = db\n    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n\n  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n  for (const member of members) {\n    createNotification(\n      member.user_id,\n      listingId,\n      \"telegram_ready\",\n      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n  await sendMessage(\n    chatId,\n    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${bookTitle}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n  );\n}\n","numLines":344,"startLine":1,"totalLines":344}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Vwmz6YgSPoGdwsBHrRQbPP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Let me analyze the findings:\n\n1. **HTML injection via book title in Telegram messages** - Line 339 in webhook route: `<b>${bookTitle}</b>` - if bookTitle contains HTML tags, they'll be interpreted. This is a real XSS/injection issue. Book titles come from Open Library API but are stored in DB, and could potentially be manipulated.\n\n2. **Discord guildId/channelId not validated** - The `guildId` and `channelId` are strings passed directly into URL paths (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). If they contain path traversal characters, they could access different API endpoints. \n\nThis is a moderate concern since it goes to Discord's API rather than our own endpoints.\n\n3. **Telegram /start listing_ID no author check** - Lines 164-169: When `/start listing_ID` is sent in a group, `linkTelegramToListing` is called without checking if the sender is the listing author. Anyone who knows a listing ID could link any Telegram group to it. This is a real issue.\n\n4. **is_full race condition** - Already covered in previous reviews partially, but checking two separate reads.\n\n5. **discord_link missing from profile reading response** - Let me check this.\n\n6. **Rate limiting on join/apply** - Already covered in hardening work (issue #61).\n\nLet me focus on the most critical vulnerabilities here.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":4105,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6111,"cache_read_input_tokens":32432,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":6111},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"a674d1fe-7170-4aac-a614-ed404677cdd6"}

  15→  member_count: number;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  author_name: string;\n    18→  joined_at: string;\n    19→}\n    20→\n    21→export async function GET() {\n    22→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    23→  if (!session) {\n    24→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    25→  }\n    26→\n    27→  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n    28→  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    29→    SELECT\n    30→      l.id,\n    31→      l.book_title,\n    32→      l.book_author,\n    33→      l.book_cover_url,\n    34→      l.reading_pace,\n    35→      l.start_date,\n    36→      l.meeting_format,\n    37→      l.is_full,\n    38→      l.telegram_link,\n    39→      l.max_group_size,\n    40→      u.display_name as author_name,\n    41→      lm.joined_at,\n    42→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    43→    FROM listing_members lm\n    44→    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    45→    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    46→    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    47→    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n    48→  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n    49→\n    50→  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n    51→\n    52→  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n    53→  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n    54→  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n    55→  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n    56→  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n    57→\n    58→  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n    59→  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n    60→  const active = readings.filter(\n    61→    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n    62→  );\n    63→\n    64→  return NextResponse.json({\n    65→    active,\n    66→    history,\n    67→    upcoming,\n    68→    total: readings.length,\n    69→  });\n 71→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"567f2b00-4ebe-4462-9bf7-14cbe2deb4bc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T00:07:11.109Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reading/route.ts","content":"import { NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\nimport db from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n\ninterface ReadingRow {\n  id: number;\n  book_title: string;\n  book_author: string;\n  book_cover_url: string;\n  reading_pace: string;\n  start_date: string;\n  meeting_format: string;\n  is_full: number;\n  telegram_link: string;\n  member_count: number;\n  max_group_size: number;\n  author_name: string;\n  joined_at: string;\n}\n\nexport async function GET() {\n  const session = await requireAuth();\n  if (!session) {\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n  }\n\n  // Get all listings the user is a member of (including ones they authored)\n  const readings = db.prepare(`\n    SELECT\n      l.id,\n      l.book_title,\n      l.book_author,\n      l.book_cover_url,\n      l.reading_pace,\n      l.start_date,\n      l.meeting_format,\n      l.is_full,\n      l.telegram_link,\n      l.max_group_size,\n      u.display_name as author_name,\n      lm.joined_at,\n      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = l.id) as member_count\n    FROM listing_members lm\n    JOIN listings l ON l.id = lm.listing_id\n    JOIN users u ON u.id = l.author_id\n    WHERE lm.user_id = ?\n    ORDER BY lm.joined_at DESC\n  `).all(session.userId) as ReadingRow[];\n\n  const today = new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0];\n\n  // Reading history: completed groups (full + start_date in the past)\n  // Upcoming: not yet started and not full\n  // Active: everything else that has started or is full (but not completed)\n  const isHistory = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 1 && r.start_date < today;\n  const isUpcoming = (r: ReadingRow) => r.is_full === 0 && r.start_date > today;\n\n  const history = readings.filter(isHistory);\n  const upcoming = readings.filter(isUpcoming);\n  const active = readings.filter(\n    (r) => !isHistory(r) && !isUpcoming(r) && (r.is_full === 1 || r.start_date <= today)\n  );\n\n  return NextResponse.json({\n    active,\n    history,\n    upcoming,\n    total: readings.length,\n  });\n}\n","numLines":71,"startLine":1,"totalLines":71}}}

1 : 0);\n    }\n    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n      values.push(platformPreference);\n    }\n\n    if (updates.length === 0) {","numLines":30,"startLine":220,"totalLines":335}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01BJBZbAABRdqeAeU8vbFLmY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Bhuukte96RH7JhvHiKBFCM","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2) && gh issue create --title \"Code review round 7: HTML injection, missing auth check, discord_link gap\" --label \"bug,P1,approved\" --body \"$(cat <<'EOF'\n## Bugs Found\n\n### 1. HTML injection in Telegram messages (CRITICAL)\n**File:** `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:339`, `lib/telegram.ts:116`\n\n`bookTitle` is interpolated directly into Telegram HTML-mode messages (`<b>${bookTitle}</b>`). If a book title contains HTML characters (e.g., `<script>`, `&`, `<`), they'll be interpreted as HTML by Telegram's parser, causing broken formatting or message injection.\n\n**Fix:** Escape HTML entities in all user-supplied content before sending via `sendMessage`.\n\n### 2. Discord guildId/channelId not validated (IMPORTANT)\n**File:** `lib/discord.ts` — `createTextChannel`, `createChannelInvite`, `sendChannelMessage`\n\n`guildId` and `channelId` from the webhook payload are used directly in URL path construction (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). Malicious input with path traversal characters could target different Discord API endpoints.\n\n**Fix:** Validate that guildId/channelId match Discord snowflake format (numeric strings).\n\n### 3. Telegram /start listing_ID has no author ownership check (IMPORTANT)\n**File:** `app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts:164-169`\n\nWhen `/start listing_ID` is sent in a group, `linkTelegramToListing` is called without verifying the sender is the listing author. Anyone in the group who knows a listing ID could hijack the link.\n\n**Fix:** Add ownership check similar to `/link` and `/link_ID` handlers.\n\n### 4. discord_link missing from profile reading response (IMPORTANT)\n**File:** `app/api/profile/reading/route.ts:28-48`\n\nThe SQL query selects `l.telegram_link` but not `l.discord_link`. Users can't see Discord invite links on their profile/reading page.\n\n**Fix:** Add `l.discord_link` to the SELECT and ReadingRow interface.\nEOF\n)\"","description":"Create GitHub issue for code review findings"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":606,"cache_read_input_tokens":44541,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":606},"output_tokens":64,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"71b3594c-3c92-4960-b469-8f6636094264","uuid":"164292b6-55d4-4d5e-87d3-8d2544bd3366"}
